# Fyzenor - Exhaustive System Reference & Technical Documentation

This document contains the complete page-by-page reference for **Fyzenor**, a lightweight, high-performance terminal user interface (TUI) file manager written in modern C++17. It details features, installation instructions, keyboard keymaps, the Trash specification, async tasks, threading architecture, theme configurator parameters, and troubleshooting guides.

---

## PAGE 1: OVERVIEW & FEATURES

### Core Philosophy
Fyzenor is built to bridge the gap between the raw power of the command line and the visual feedback of modern GUIs. It uses a decoupled multi-threaded architecture to ensure heavy I/O operations never block the user interface.

### Key Features
1. **Three-Column Miller Layout**:
   - Left Column: Pinned Bookmarks panel.
   - Middle Column: Active browser pane showing directories, metadata, file sizes, and modification dates.
   - Right Column: Live previews showing code snippets, video/audio details, file information, and images (via Kitty Graphics Protocol).
2. **Asynchronous I/O Offloading**:
   - Copying, moving, zipping, extraction, and trashing operations are offloaded to background threads (`std::thread`).
   - NCurses TUI drawing loops run completely non-blocking, maintaining 0ms input latency even during heavy bulk operations.
3. **Smart Copy Resumption (Delta Overwrite)**:
   - Tracks interrupted transfers and resumes writing block-by-block.
   - If conflicts arise, prompts choices: `[r]eplace`, `[o]verwrite if older`, `[s]kip`, or `[a]sk`.
4. **Compliance-Tested Multi-Partition Trash**:
   - Complies with Freedesktop.org Desktop Trash Can Specification.
   - Uses zero-copy local renames on the same partition, falling back to secure permanent deletion prompts when trash directories are unsupported (FAT/NTFS/Read-Only mounts).
5. **Block Device Mounts**:
   - Built-in overlay to mount and browse external USB flash drives and Android mobile phones (MTP).
6. **Global Shell Macros**:
   - Run terminal commands locally inside directories. Use `$f` for the highlighted file path, `$s` for selected item buffers, and `&` at the end to execute commands in the background without UI blockages.

---

## PAGE 2: QUICK START & INSTALLATION

### 1. Dependencies
Before compiling or running Fyzenor, install the required packages for your system:

#### Debian / Ubuntu / Mint
```bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt install build-essential libncursesw5-dev ffmpeg zip bat xclip wl-copy ripgrep
```

#### Fedora / RedHat
```bash
sudo dnf update
sudo dnf install gcc gcc-c++ make ncurses-devel ffmpeg zip bat xclip wl-clipboard ripgrep
```

#### Arch Linux / Manjaro
```bash
sudo pacman -Sy
sudo pacman -S base-devel ncurses ffmpeg zip bat xclip wl-clipboard ripgrep
```

#### Android (Termux)
```bash
pkg update
pkg install clang cmake ndk-sysroot ncurses-utils ffmpeg zip bat ripgrep
```

---

### 2. Installation Script
Fyzenor provides an automated setup script that detects Termux vs. standard Linux environments, handles prefix variables, bypasses `sudo` prompts when appropriate, builds from source, and installs the binary to path:
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Bimbok/fyzenor/main/install.sh | bash
```

---

### 3. Manual Compilation
To compile manually from source:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/Bimbok/fyzenor.git
cd fyzenor
cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
cmake --build build
# Run local binary
./fyzenor
```

---

### 4. Installation Troubleshooting
- **Filesystem Compilation Errors**:
  If compiling on GCC versions older than GCC 9, append `-lstdc++fs` to link libraries manually if `std::filesystem` is missing.
- **NCurses Locale & Icons Clashing**:
  If icons or folders render as strange glyphs (e.g. `?` or block characters), verify your system locale supports wide characters:
  ```bash
  locale
  ```
  Ensure your active shell environment variables set `LANG=en_US.UTF-8` or equivalent.
- **Nerd Font Requirement**:
  Fyzenor renders folder and file icons using Nerd Font glyphs. Configure your terminal application to use any Nerd Font (e.g. FiraCode, Hack, or JetBrainsMono Nerd Font) to display TUI icons cleanly.

---

## PAGE 3: KEYBOARD CONTROLS & SHORTCUTS

Fyzenor employs Vim-style keystrokes for efficient, mouse-free directory navigation and file manipulation.

### Category: Navigation
- `h` : Navigates back to the parent directory.
- `j` : Moves highlighted selection down.
- `k` : Moves highlighted selection up.
- `l` : Opens current file in editor (respects `$EDITOR` variable) or enters highlighted directory.
- `g` : Jumps instantly to the top of the active file listing.
- `G` : Jumps instantly to the bottom of the active file listing.
- `Tab` : Switches focus between Bookmarks sidebar list and active directory panel.

### Category: Selection
- `Space` : Toggles selection state of highlighted file or folder.
- `v` : Toggles selection state of highlighted file or folder.
- `a` : Selects all files and folders in the current directory view.

### Category: File Operations
- `d` : Moves all selected/highlighted items to Trash (runs asynchronously in a background thread).
- `D` : Permanently deletes items (bypasses Trash).
- `u` : Undoes last Trash action, restoring items to original absolute paths.
- `c` : Copies highlighted item's absolute path to clipboard.
- `y` : Yanks (copies) selected/highlighted items to clipboard buffer.
- `x` : Cuts selected/highlighted items to clipboard buffer.
- `p` : Pastes items (spawns async task manager worker).
- `Shift+Y` : Pastes absolute symbolic links pointing to yanked files.
- `r` : Rename current file / Bulk rename selected items / Restore highlighted items if inside Trash Manager.
- `n` : Creates new empty file in current directory.
- `N` : Creates new directory folder.
- `z` : Compresses selected items into a `.zip` archive asynchronously.
- `e` : Extracts highlighted archive / Empties partition trash bins if inside Trash Manager.

### Category: Views & Overlays
- `w` : Opens the Asynchronous Task Manager.
- `T` : Opens the Trash Manager.
- `m` : Opens Block Devices & MTP Mounts manager.
- `F2` : Reloads current directory / Refreshes mount listings.
- `s` : Cycles sorting modes: Name -> Size (Desc) -> Date Modified (Desc).
- `P` : Pins current directory path to bookmarks.
- `:` : Launches global shell command prompt.
- `?` : Opens help menu keymap overlay.
- `Esc` : Closes active overlay window / cancels search.

---

## PAGE 4: TRASH DEEP DIVE

### Architecture Overview
Fyzenor incorporates a Freedesktop.org Desktop Trash Can Specification compliant subsystem to manage deletions safely. 

1. **Partition-Local Trash Folders**:
   To avoid slow, byte-copying operations when deleting on external drives (USB sticks, SSD partitions, external mounts), Fyzenor moves files to:
   - `<mount_root>/.Trash-<uid>/files/`
   Associated metadata files are written in standard formats to:
   - `<mount_root>/.Trash-<uid>/info/`
   This leverages the filesystem's instant constant-time metadata rename `std::filesystem::rename`.
2. **Primary Drive Home Trash**:
   Deletions on the primary partition move files to:
   - `~/.local/share/Trash/files/`
   - `~/.local/share/Trash/info/[file].trashinfo`

### Metadata Format (`.trashinfo` Specification)
Each deleted item creates a matching `.trashinfo` metadata file containing path translations and deletion timestamps:
```ini
[Trash Info]
Path=/home/bimbok/shared/important_docs/invoice.pdf
DeletionDate=2026-07-05T20:14:05
```

### Fallback Permanent Deletion
On filesystems lacking UNIX file permission flags (FAT, NTFS, or read-only mounts), local trash directories cannot be initialized. Fyzenor catches this error state and prompts:
`"Trash not supported. Delete permanently? (y/n)"`

### Asynchronous AsyncTask Workers
Move-to-trash operations are handled by background threads. When bulk-deleting large collections, a thread worker performs standard folder traversals and moves in the background, preventing UI lockups.

---

## PAGE 5: TASK CONTROLS & SMART COPY

### 1. Active Task Manager (`w`)
Pressing `w` displays all background operations. Supported actions:
- **Copy**: Custom block-level readers/writers.
- **Deletion**: Standard C++ filesystem remove loops.
- **Trash**: Partition-local moves.
- **Zip / Extract**: External tar/zip subprocesses.

#### Process Controls
- **Pause**: Sends POSIX signal `SIGSTOP` to subprocesses or suspends block iteration loop via conditional variables (consuming 0% CPU).
- **Resume**: Sends POSIX signal `SIGCONT` or signals block threads to resume.
- **Cancel**: Kills threads and cleans up partial remnants.

---

### 2. Smart Copy Resumption (Delta Overwrites)
If a copy task is cancelled, incomplete destination files remain. Pasting the items again prompts a smart resolution dialog:
1. `[r]eplace` : Overwrites destination file entirely.
2. `[o]verwrite if older` : Compares timestamps and overwrites only if source modification date is newer than destination.
3. `[s]kip` : Skips copying the file.
4. `[a]sk` : Prompts resolution on every conflict.

---

## PAGE 6: ARCHITECTURE & THREADS CONCURRENCY

Fyzenor separates rendering loops from heavy file I/O operations through a strict thread boundary.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[NCurses Main UI Thread] -->|Registers task & spawns| B(std::thread AsyncTask Worker)
    B -->|Performs heavy I/O operations| C[Filesystem / External Disk]
    B -->|Updates task registry| D(activeTasks Registry)
    A -->|Periodically polls| D
    D -->|Updates finished state| A
    A -->|Triggers reload & redraws TUI| E[Terminal Screen]
```

### Ncurses Thread boundary Rules
1. NCurses redraw methods (`wrefresh`, `mvwprintw`, `clear`) must only run on the main UI thread. 
2. Background worker threads communicate progress exclusively by updating status flags inside the thread-safe `activeTasks` registry.
3. The main UI loop polls the registry, clears finished tasks, and triggers safe, crash-free interface reloads.

---

## PAGE 7: THEME CONFIGURATOR

Fyzenor matches custom skins and terminal Matugen templates by parsing colors from:
`~/.config/fyzenor/colors.fz`

### Custom Theme Variables (19 C++ Parameters)
- `bg` : Terminal background color.
- `border` : Border lines separating panels.
- `statusbar` : Background for active status bars.
- `activeText` : Selected text and highlight details.
- `normalText` : Standard file and folder listings.
- `selectedBg` : Highlight background capsule in directories.
- `selectedFg` : Text color inside selected highlight capsules.
- `accentGlow` : Primary glowing accents (shadows/highlights).
- `accentPurple` : Second highlighting color.
- `accentCyan` : Third highlighting color.
- `accentOrange` : Warnings and mounts colors.
- `accentRed` : Critical alerts and error prompts.
- `fileDir` : Directory folder icons and text.
- `fileReg` : Regular files.
- `fileExe` : Executable scripts and binaries.
- `fileLnk` : Symbolic link files.
- `fileZip` : Zip, tar, and gz compressed archives.
- `fileMedia` : Images, audio, and video files.
- `fileOthers` : Unidentified file categories.

---

## PAGE 8: CUSTOM KEYBOARD MACROS & RICH PREVIEWS

### 1. Custom Keyboard Macros (`keys.fz`)
Map single-key shortcuts to run shell commands on highlighted/selected items.
- **Config Path**: `~/.config/fyzenor/keys.fz` (auto-generated if missing).
- **Format**: `key=command` (e.g. `v=nvim "$f"`).
- **Placeholders**: `$f` (highlighted path), `$s` (space-separated selected paths).
- **Execution**: Suspends NCurses, changes directory to the browsed folder, runs in foreground interactive terminal, and prompts Enter to return.

### 2. Archive Tree Previewer
Asynchronously lists files within compressed archives:
- **Formats**: `.zip`, `.tar.gz`, `.tgz`, `.7z`, `.rar`, `.tar`, `.bz2`, `.xz`.
- **Backend Commands**: Runs `unzip -l`, `7z l`, `unrar l`, or `tar -tf` via `popen` background loops.

### 3. Media Metadata Inspector
Asynchronously reads metadata headers of images, audio, and video files:
- **Backend Probes**: Uses `mediainfo` or `ffprobe` to list tracks info (resolution, bitrate, sample rate, codec name, title, artist).

---

- **Github**: Explore codebase, file issues, submit forks, and contribute pull requests:
  `git@github.com:Bimbok/fyzenor.git`
